name: Week 1 Pull (Back/Biceps/Rear Delts)
description: Foundation pull day grouped by station — pull-up bar, then barbell rows, then all cable work, then dumbbells to finish. Minimizes equipment switching. All fixed exercises.

exercises.notes: Squeeze shoulder blades, warm up upper back
Decompress spine, activate grip and lats
Full dead hang to chin over bar. Add weight when you hit 3×8. If you can't do 5, use a band or do negatives.
Hinge at hips, pull to lower chest, squeeze at top. Keep core tight, don't round the back.
Wide grip, lean back slightly, pull to upper chest. Control the eccentric.
Swap to close-grip handle. Chest up, pull to belly button, squeeze shoulder blades.
High cable, rope attachment. Pull to face, externally rotate at the end. Light weight.
Low cable, straight bar or EZ bar. Full extension, squeeze at top. Finish all cable work here.
Bent over on a bench or standing. Slight bend in elbows, squeeze rear delts.
Same dumbbells, neutral grip. Hits brachialis and forearms.

tips: Grouped by station — pull-up bar → barbell → cable machine → dumbbells
Cable station has 4 exercises — just swap attachments and weight, don't move
Pull-ups and rows are your main lifts — progressive overload these
Face pulls are non-negotiable — they keep your shoulders healthy for pressing days
Total volume: ~18 working sets for pull muscles
